name: Ninja Creami NC301 Easy No-Cook Tres Leches (US)
author: Ice Cream Science
comments: Source: https://www.icecreamscience.com/blog/ninja-creami-nc30-easy-no-cook-tres-leches-recipe, visited 2026-07-06. Sci-Cream's composition of the ingredients used in the recipe do not agree with Ice Cream Science's composition - milk is 3.23% vs 3.6% fat, cream is 33.4% vs 32.4% fat, and most importantly, sweetened condensed milk is 46% vs ~58% sucrose. As a result, the mix composition differs significantly from Ice Cream Science's composition, with total solids 41.1% vs 46.34%, MSNF 10.9% vs 12.16%, fat 15.5% vs 16.02%, and sucrose 14.5% vs 18.15%. This is the US version of the recipe; see "Ninja Creami NC301 Easy No-Cook Tres Leches (UK)" for the UK version. This is a no-cook recipe intended to be made using the Ninja Creami NC301 ice cream maker; I am not sure how it would perform in a standard ice cream maker.

name: Ninja Creami NC301 Easy No-Cook Tres Leches (UK)
author: Ice Cream Science
comments: Source: https://www.icecreamscience.com/blog/ninja-creami-nc30-easy-no-cook-tres-leches-recipe, visited 2026-07-06. Sci-Cream's resulting mix composition matches Ice Cream Science's stated composition to within ~5%. This is the UK version of the recipe; see "Ninja Creami NC301 Easy No-Cook Tres Leches (US)" for the US version. This is a no-cook recipe intended to be made using the Ninja Creami NC301 ice cream maker; I am not sure how it would perform in a standard ice cream maker.
